title: Network Security and Cryptography
content:
Cryptography – Hinglish Notes (Line by Line)

The art or science encompassing the principles and methods of
transforming an intelligible message into one that is unintelligible,
and then retransforming that message back to its original form

1. Introduction

  • Is presentation/session me Cryptography ke baare me discuss kiya jaata hai.

  • Session complete hone ke baad aap:

    • Symmetric Cipher Model samajh paoge.

    • Goals aur types of cryptography samajh paoge.

    • Encryption aur Decryption ki importance samajh paoge.

    • Unconditional security aur Computational security ke baare me jaan paoge.


2. Basic Concepts in Cryptography

Cryptography me kuch important terms hote hain:

1. Plain Text

  • Plain text original message hota hai.

  • Ye wahi data hota hai jo sender bhejna chahta hai.

Example
Message:

"Hello Ajay"

Ye plain text hai.

2. Cipher Text

  • Jab plain text ko encrypt kiya jaata hai to wo cipher text ban jaata hai.

  • Cipher text unintelligible (samajh me na aane wala) hota hai.

Example

Plain Text

HELLO

Cipher Text

XKDJ92$

3. Encryption Algorithm

  • Encryption algorithm ek method hai jo plain text ko cipher text me convert karta hai.

  • Is process me key use hoti hai.

Process:

Plain Text + Key
→ Encryption Algorithm
→ Cipher Text

4. Decryption Algorithm

  • Decryption algorithm cipher text ko wapas plain text me convert karta hai.

Process:

Cipher Text + Key
→ Decryption Algorithm
→ Plain Text

5. Sender aur Receiver Communication

Cryptography me communication process:

  1. Sender ke paas plain text hota hai.

  2. Sender encryption algorithm + key use karta hai.

  3. Message cipher text me convert ho jaata hai.

  4. Cipher text internet/channel ke through bheja jaata hai.

  5. Receiver cipher text receive karta hai.

  6. Receiver decryption algorithm + key use karta hai.

  7. Original plain text wapas mil jaata hai.

6. Role of Channel

  • Channel internet ya network hota hai jahan se data travel karta hai.

  • Is channel par attackers bhi ho sakte hain.

  • Isliye message plain text me nahi, cipher text me bheja jaata hai.

7. Importance of Key

  • Cryptography me key sabse important element hai.

  • Encryption algorithm public ho sakta hai.

  • Lekin key secret honi chahiye.

Important Point
Security algorithm par nahi, key par depend karti hai.

Example

Agar attacker ko pata ho:

  • encryption algorithm kya hai

Lekin agar key nahi pata
→ wo message nahi samajh sakta.

8. Why Key Must Be Secret

Agar attacker:

  • Cipher text dekh le

  • Encryption algorithm bhi jaan le

Tab bhi:

Agar key nahi pata
→ message decode nahi kar sakta.

Isliye:

Key = Most Critical Information

9. Why We Send Cipher Text

Internet par bahut log data access kar sakte hain.

Isliye:

Plain text bhejna risky hai.

Solution:

Plain Text
→ Encryption
→ Cipher Text
→ Internet

Result:

Koi bhi message ko samajh nahi paayega.

10. History Idea of Cryptography

Cryptography ka main goal:

Secret messages ko safe tarike se send karna.

Process:

Sender side:
Plain Text
→ Encryption
→ Cipher Text

Receiver side:
Cipher Text
→ Decryption
→ Plain Text

11. Attackers in Cryptography

Internet par attackers:

  • Cipher text intercept kar sakte hain

  • Cipher break karne ki koshish karte hain

Lekin:

Agar secret key safe hai
→ message secure rahega.

14. Definition of Cryptography

Cryptography ki definition:

Cryptography is the art and science of transforming an intelligible message into an unintelligible message.

Meaning:

Cryptography ek technique hai jisme:

Plain Text (samajh me aane wala message)
→ Cipher Text (samajh me na aane wala message)

banaya jaata hai.

15. Reverse Process

Receiver side par:

Cipher Text
→ Decryption
→ Plain Text

Is process se message wapas original form me aa jaata hai.

16. Types of Cryptography

Cryptography ke 2 main types hote hain:

1. Symmetric Cryptography

2. Asymmetric Cryptography

17. Symmetric Cryptography

Isme:

  • Same key encryption aur decryption ke liye use hoti hai.

Isko Private Key Cryptography bhi kehte hain.

18. Asymmetric Cryptography

Isme:

  • Different keys use hoti hain.

2 keys hoti hain:

1️⃣ Public Key
2️⃣ Private Key

19. Public Key

  • Public key sabko pata ho sakti hai.

  • Isse encryption kiya jaata hai.

  • lock koo bhi lgaskta h 

20. Private Key

  • Private key secret hoti hai.

  • Isse decryption kiya jaata hai.

  • unlock koi koi krskta h 

21. Encryption Schemes

Encryption schemes ka matlab:

Message ko encrypt karne ka method.

Encryption scheme ki 2 important properties hoti hain:

1️⃣ Unconditional Security
2️⃣ Computational Security

22. Unconditionally Secure Encryption

Encryption scheme unconditionally secure tab hoti hai jab:

Cipher text me itni information na ho ki plain text ko determine kiya ja sake.

does not have enough information by which we can uniquely define ki iska plain text yeh hoga 

Even if:

  • attacker ke paas unlimited computational power

  • unlimited time

Tab bhi:

Wo plain text nahi nikal paata.

23. Example of Unconditional Security

Attacker:

  • Cipher text receive karta hai

  • Guess karne ki koshish karta hai

Lekin:

Unlimited time aur resources hone ke baad bhi

→ Plain text nahi pata lagta.

Isliye system unconditionally secure hai.

24. Computationally Secure Encryption

invested a lot of time in breking the cipher text still woh succesful nhi hota ise khte h computationally secure 

Encryption scheme computationally secure tab hoti hai jab:

Attacker theoretically break kar sakta hai
but practically bahut difficult hota hai.

25. Conditions of Computational Security

Encryption scheme computationally secure tab hoti hai jab:

Condition 1

Cipher break karne ka cost

encrypted information ki value se zyada ho.

Example:

Data value = ₹1000
Breaking cost = ₹10,00,000

Attacker try nahi karega.

Condition 2

Cipher break karne ka time

information ki useful lifetime se zyada ho.

Example:

Password valid hai = 2 days

Break karne ka time = 5 years

To attacker ke liye useless hai.

Security Mechanisms – Notes

1. Introduction

In the previous topic we studied Security Attacks and Security Services.
In this topic we study Security Mechanisms.

Security Architecture

Security architecture mainly consists of three components:

  1. Security Attacks

  2. Security Mechanisms

  3. Security Services

  • Security services implement security policies.

  • Data and system resources are protected using security mechanisms.

Definition of Security Mechanism

A Security Mechanism is a technique or method used to implement security services and protect system resources and data.

Security mechanisms ensure that security policies are correctly applied in a system.

Types of Security Mechanisms

There are two types:

  1. Specific Security Mechanisms

  2. Pervasive Security Mechanisms

1. Specific Security Mechanisms

These mechanisms are designed to provide specific security services.

1. Encryption

Encryption is used to provide data confidentiality.

It converts:

  • Plain Text → Cipher Text

Encryption uses strong mathematical algorithms and keys.

Purpose

  • Protect data from unauthorized access

  • Ensure only authorized users can read the information

2. Digital Signature

A Digital Signature is a piece of code attached to a message.

It works like a real signature to verify the sender’s identity.

Functions

  • Provides Authentication

  • Ensures Data Integrity

The receiver can verify that:

  • The message came from the correct sender

  • The message has not been modified

3. Access Control

Access control determines who can access system resources.

Not all users have the same level of access.

Example

In a banking system:

  • Manager → Full access

  • Employee → Limited access

  • Customer → Access only to their own account

4. Data Integrity

Data integrity ensures that data is not modified during transmission.

The receiver must receive exactly the same data that was sent.

If an attacker modifies the data, the system should be able to detect the modification.

5. Authentication Exchange

Authentication exchange is done through information exchange between communicating parties.

Small pieces of information are exchanged periodically to confirm that the entities are legitimate.

Purpose

  • Verify the identity of communicating entities

  • Prevent unauthorized users from participating in communication

6. Traffic Padding

Traffic padding is used to prevent traffic analysis attacks.

Explanation

Sometimes when no data is transmitted, an attacker may detect that the network is idle.

To prevent this:

  • Random data is sent even when no real data is transmitted.

This keeps the traffic flow constant and hides real communication patterns.

7. Routing Control

Routing control determines the path used to send data through the network.

Data is transmitted through secure and trusted routes.

Purpose

  • Avoid compromised network paths

  • Ensure secure data transmission

8. Notarization

Notarization involves a trusted third party that verifies communication between two parties.

The third party helps resolve disputes and provide proof of communication.

Example

  • Certificate Authorities (CA)

  • Digital Certificates (SSL Certificates)

These confirm the authenticity of communication.

2. Pervasive Security Mechanisms

These mechanisms are general security functions and are not limited to a specific service or layer.

1. Trusted Functionality

Trusted functionality enforces security policies in a system.

It ensures that system actions follow defined security rules and criteria.

2. Security Labels

Security labels are tags assigned to system resources or data.

They indicate:

  • Sensitivity level

  • Required protection level

Example:
Confidential, Secret, Public.

3. Event Detection

Event detection monitors activities happening in the system.

It identifies:

  • Normal events

  • Suspicious or malicious activities

This helps detect potential attacks or unauthorized access.

4. Security Audit

Security audit is the review of system records and activities.

Logs and records are analyzed to:

  • Detect security violations

  • Verify system security

5. Security Recovery

Security recovery focuses on restoring the system after a security failure or attack.

If an attack damages the system:

  • Recovery mechanisms restore the system to a secure state.

Summary

Specific Security Mechanisms

  1. Encryption

  2. Digital Signature

  3. Access Control

  4. Data Integrity

  5. Authentication Exchange

  6. Traffic Padding

  7. Routing Control

  8. Notarization

Pervasive Security Mechanisms

  1. Trusted Functionality

  2. Security Labels

  3. Event Detection

  4. Security Audit

  5. Security Recovery
